hero.title: One console for your whole local agent stack
hero.sub: A single Zig binary with an embedded Svelte dashboard that installs, supervises, monitors and updates NullClaw, NullBoiler, NullTickets and NullWatch. All state lives under ~/.nullhub — nothing goes to a hosted service.

quickstart:
Start the server, then add components from the same binary — the terminal wizard and the web UI run the same manifest-driven flow.

```bash
# start the server and open the dashboard
nullhub
# → http://nullhub.localhost:19800

# add components (terminal wizard)
nullhub install nullclaw
nullhub install nullwatch

# run and inspect
nullhub start-all
nullhub status
```

features.title: Manifest-driven install wizard
features.body: Each component publishes a nullhub-manifest.json describing its installation, wizard steps, launch and health checks. NullHub is a generic engine that interprets them — in the terminal or the browser.

features.title: Process supervision
features.body: Start, stop, restart, crash recovery with backoff. Periodic HTTP health checks feed the dashboard status cards.

features.title: Updates with rollback
features.body: check-updates, update, update-all: download the new version, migrate config, roll back on failure.

features.title: Live logs over SSE
features.body: Tail or follow logs per instance — nullhub logs <c>/<n> -f in the terminal, live streaming in the browser.

features.title: Multi-instance
features.body: Run several instances of the same component side by side. Everything is addressed as component/instance-name.

features.title: Web UI, CLI and raw API
features.body: Browser dashboard for humans, CLI for automation, and nullhub api for raw JSON access to every endpoint.

note: Pre-1.0 and calendar-versioned (v2026.x.y): config and CLI may change between releases.
